headline: 🥈 Senior AI | ML Engineer | Open Source Contributor (NVIDIA, Keras and more) | Expertise in CV, NLP, and RL
about:
In 2018, I started my Master's in Data Science with the belief that AI will revolutionize the way humans live and work and data is at the core of it, and here we are where we have started seeing the impact of AI on our lives.

I am glad to be a part of this change where we teach machines to see, understand, and respond intelligently. Over the past five years, I’ve worked on building NLP and Computer Vision solutions that solve real-world problems, from creating smarter chatbots to developing high-performance segmentation models. Every project has been an opportunity to learn, innovate, and contribute to the growth of AI.

On the NLP side, I have worked with LangChain, LLM agents, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). My expertise spans fine-tuning classification and generation models, and I’ve also developed streaming speech-to-text and text-to-speech solutions for real-time processing. On the Computer Vision side, I have worked on object detection and tracking systems, alongside implementing advanced segmentation techniques like deep image matting and salient object detection. Deployment is another area of strength, where I’ve leveraged tools like TensorRT, NVIDIA Triton Server, and Docker to create scalable and efficient AI applications.

Also proud to contribute to the open-source community. I have worked on projects for KerasCV, Keras.io, and NVIDIA, adding advanced models and making research papers more accessible. Sharing knowledge is a big part of what drives me, and it's rewarding to see others benefit from these contributions.

That’s it, Last I want to say I am always ready to learn, help, and guide others. AI is a field of endless possibilities, and I am thrilled to play a role in shaping its future.

currentPosition.description:
Client: SummitK12
AI-Powered Automated Grading & Evaluation Platform
- Built an AI system to automatically grade student answers, handling ~150K+ evaluations daily.
- The AI SDK takes questions, student answers, media, and rubrics as input, and returns scores and feedback based on educator-defined rubrics.
- Designed the system to support millions of students and evaluations, handling high request volumes with low latency.
- Used AWS (Lambda, Bedrock) with LiteLLM to handle models, retries, and failures.
- Added logging and monitoring to track issues and debug problems.
- Tested system performance under heavy load and improved response times.
- Focused on keeping the system stable and reliable for real classroom use.

experience.description:
Client: SummitK12
AI-Powered Automated Grading & Evaluation Platform
- Built an AI system to automatically grade student answers, handling ~150K+ evaluations daily.
- The AI SDK takes questions, student answers, media, and rubrics as input, and returns scores and feedback based on educator-defined rubrics.
- Designed the system to support millions of students and evaluations, handling high request volumes with low latency.
- Used AWS (Lambda, Bedrock) with LiteLLM to handle models, retries, and failures.
- Added logging and monitoring to track issues and debug problems.
- Tested system performance under heavy load and improved response times.
- Focused on keeping the system stable and reliable for real classroom use.

• Developed a ChatBot using RASA, LangChain Agents, OpenAI GPT API, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), automating 30% of customer queries and assisting support agents with 30% more pre-selected responses, reducing workload and improving SMS support efficiency.
• Developed a Blood Test Recommendation System leveraging query optimization with multiple queries in RAG to analyze user inputs (symptoms, goals) and retrieve relevant tests, streamlining search processes to boost sales.
• Created a Reddit bot using PRAW Python and LLM prompt engineering, boosting our brand’s presence on the platform.
• Organized textual data, including the use and finetuning of transformers models for text classification and generation. Implemented streaming speech-to-text (STT) and text-to-speech (TTS) solutions, enabling real-time data processing. Additionally, applied embedding techniques for text retrieval and clustering, collaborating with vector databases.
• Analyzed data to understand sales trends, optimize Google Ads campaigns, and keywords for better campaign performance.

Contributed UNet3+ implementation with complete training and inference pipeline, along with below mentioned four features, to NVIDIA repository.
- NVIDIA DALI
- Multi GPU Training
- Accelerated Linear Algebra(XLA)
- Automatic Mixed Precision(AMP)

Contribution Link: https://github.com/NVIDIA/DeepLearningExamples/tree/master/TensorFlow2/Segmentation/Contrib/UNet3P

Worked on an NLP project called News Revealer: A platform for news generation in which users can summarise, paraphrase, and generate articles. 
Deployed transformers based models using Flask, Docker, NGINX, TensorRT, and NVIDIA Triton Server on DGX machines. Resulted in 1.5x to 2.2x improvement in throughput.
Finetuned transformer model for unsupervised text style transfer.

Worked on two Computer Vision and one Data Science project.

Nails Try On: A segmentation based task in which using a state of the art real time custom segmentation model I was able to achieve 13% improvement in accuracy and around 20% improvement in terms of speed.

Beauty: An image to image translation task in which by changing input flow I was able to relatively reduce 30% overfitting in existing cGAN and Pix2Pix GAN model.

Luccro: A decision support engine for financial trading(Forex) that uses AI to identify low-risk, high-reward opportunities for traders. Generated trading indicators, and worked on model training and deployment of Technical and Strength time series data.

experience.companyName: Trust for Democratic Education and Accountability

Parliamentary Proceedings: Application designed to observe the proceedings of the Senate, National Assembly, and 13 selected Standing Committees under project Parliament Watch and Reforms Project (PWRP).

FAFEN Observation: A Survey Application that collected the data of the 2018 General Elections held in Pakistan under Citizens' Voice Project (CVP).

Pooling Station Survey: An android application was developed for Election Commission of Pakistan to collect data on potential pooling stations.

projects.description: Contributed state-of-the-art BASNet model for salient and camouflaged object segmentation in Keras examples repository.

projects.title: Object Counter
projects.description: The goal was to count objects passing over the conveyer belt using edge devices with at least 10 FPS. Quantized Tiny Yolo-v4 was used for detection followed by OpenCV provided MOSSE tracker for counting purposes. Overall, we achieved good accuracy with the required speed.

projects.title: Incident Severity Scoring
projects.description: The goal was to estimate incident severity from given articles. It was open ended problem so different approaches were tested like Rule based classification, training Logistic Regression and SVM, fine tuning BERT and XLNET. After performing upsampling and summarizing articles with BERT extractive summarizer, fine-tuned XLNET model was selected because of best results. This project was implemented in PyTorch with pre-trained “xlnet-base-cased” model provided by Hugging Face library.

publications.title: Reducing Entropy Overestimation in Soft Actor Critic Using Dual Policy Network
publications.description: Recently proposed Reinforcement learning algorithm Soft Actor Critic (SAC) maximizes entropy along with expected discounted rewards to encourage exploration. Entropy is maximized to discourage agents from creating high peak probabilities for some action. In this research, we found that entropy is only needed where high peaks are developed for some action. Adding entropy in every state causes unstable and slow policy learning. To overcome this, we proposed to use dual policy SAC in which both policies tries to maximize entropy to encourage exploration but chooses action against minimum entropy which resulted in stable and fast learning.

headline: AI Engineering & DevRel Leader, Recently: Director, Google Cloud AI. Best-selling Author. Speaker. AI, DX, UX. I want to see you win.
about: Addy Osmani is an Irish Software Engineer and leader who was most recently a director at Google Cloud AI, focused on helping developers and businesses succeed with Gemini, Agent Platform, and the Agent Development Kit (ADK). After nearly 14 years at Google leading developer experiences in Chrome, he bridged Google DeepMind, engineering, product, and developer relations teams. A developer for 25+ years, he is passionate about AI-assisted engineering and developer tools. Follow for motivation, productivity and engineering.

Between my Medium channel and personal site, the canonical technical articles I author have been viewed more than 17M times in the last few years. Gemini! Achieved Cloud AI's annual growth goal in <6 months. My team focused on empowering developers and businesses to succeed with Google's AI platform. This includes AI models like Gemini, Agent Platform (ADK, Agent Studio etc) to name a few.

Our teams work on reducing the friction to test, debug and optimize web applications in the developer's preferred web technology and tooling stack. Portfolio includes products reaching 40M developers serving a Chrome audience of 3B+ users.

My AI specific projects have included: Contributing to Gemini model improvements for coding, Gemini (AI Assistance) in Chrome DevTools across 5 different product launches and Gemini rollouts across multiple developer surfaces in Google Labs.

Engineering manager leading organisations working on making Chrome & the web fast. My work involves leading some of our projects to help how quickly Chrome loads pages, shaping APIs, and defining best practices to help large sites deliver great user experiences.

projects.title: Chrome DevTools
projects.description: Chrome DevTools is a suite of web development tools used by over 30M developers integrated directly into the Google Chrome browser, designed to assist developers in editing pages on-the-fly, diagnosing issues swiftly, and building better websites more efficiently.

projects.title: Google Chrome
projects.description:
Google Chrome is a popular web browser we developed over time, first released in 2008. It is known for its speed, simplicity, security, and stability, often referred to as the four S principles:

1. Speed: Chrome is designed for efficiency and quick navigation, with a fast startup and page loading time.

2. Simplicity: The browser offers a clean, intuitive user interface, making it easy for users to navigate and manage their browsing experience.

3. Security: Chrome prioritizes user safety with features like safe browsing, sandboxing, and regular updates to protect against threats.

4. Stability: It runs each tab independently, so if one crashes, it doesn't affect the rest of the browser, ensuring a more stable browsing experience.

projects.title: WebComponents.org
projects.description: Googlers from the Polymer/Chrome team, along with some great folks from Mozilla’s X-Tag/Brick, as well as other Web Components community members (like me) had been cooking up a vision for a simple, neutral site/community devoted to encouraging good best practices for Web Components.

publications.title: Success at Scale
publications.description:
Success at Scale is a curated collection of best-practice case studies capturing how production sites of different sizes tackle performance, accessibility, capabilities, and developer experience at scale. 

Case studies are from industry experts from teams at Instagram, Shopify, Netflix, eBay, Figma, Spotify, Wix, Lyft, LinkedIn, and many more. Guidance that stands the test of time.

Join Addy Osmani, your curator, as we dive into a nuanced look at several key topics that will teach you tips and tricks that may help you optimize your own sites. The case studies fall into four major categories that impact almost every project — Performance, Capabilities, Accessibility, and Developer Experience — and the book also includes short interviews with contributors on what additional lessons, challenges, and tips they have to share some time after the case studies were written.

Rather than focusing on conventional project management steps and procedures, Success at Scale is about the ideas and processes that worked (and a few that didn’t), capturing challenges, frustrations, and wins through the eyes of the people who make change happen.

publications.title: Leading Effective Engineering Teams
publications.description:
In this insightful and comprehensive guide, Addy Osmani shares more than a decade of experience working on the Chrome team at Google, uncovering secrets to engineering effectiveness, efficiency, and team success. Engineers and engineering leaders looking to scale their effectiveness and drive transformative results within their teams and organizations will learn the essential principles, tips, and frameworks for building highly effective engineering teams.

Osmani presents best practices and proven strategies that foster engineering excellence in organizations of all sizes. Through practical advice and real-world examples, Leading Effective Engineering Teams empowers you to create a thriving engineering culture where individuals and teams can excel. Unlock the full potential of your engineering team and achieve unparalleled success by harnessing the power of trust, commitment, and accountability.

With this book, you'll discover:

The essential traits for engineering effectiveness and the pitfalls to avoid
How to cultivate trust, commitment, and accountability within your team
Strategies to minimize friction, optimize career growth, and deliver maximum value
The dynamics of highly successful engineering teams and how to replicate their achievements
How to implement a systems thinking approach for everyday problem-solving and decision-making
Self-advocacy techniques to enhance your team's visibility and recognition within the organization

publications.title: The Stoic Mind - A Visual Exploration Of Stoic Philosophy
publications.description:
Discover the timeless wisdom of Stoicism in a modern context with "The Stoic Mind," an enlightening visual guide by GoLimitless and Addy Osmani. This rich exploration connects ancient philosophical tenets to modern-day scenarios, offering you a compass to navigate life's challenging waves with resilience and inner peace.

Whether it's about stepping out of your comfort zone, taking control of your calendar, measuring success by your own standards, or understanding the multifaceted nature of success and failure, this book offers a treasure trove of insights, each steeped in the enduring wisdom of Stoicism.

Remember, the journey is about progress, not perfection. As you turn the pages of "The Stoic Mind," you equip yourself with the tools to sail through life's vast ocean with fortitude, irrespective of the storms you face. These teachings aren't relics of the past but beacons of wisdom for the present and future.

publications.title: Learning JavaScript Design Patterns: Second Edition (2023)
publications.description:
Do you want to write beautiful, structured, and maintainable JavaScript by applying modern design patterns to the language? Do you want clean, efficient, manageable code? Want to stay up-to-date with the latest best practices? If so, the updated second edition of Learning JavaScript Design Patterns is the ideal place to start.

Author Addy Osmani shows you how to apply modern design patterns to JavaScript and React—including modules, mixins, observers, and mediators. You'll learn about performance and rendering patterns such as server-side rendering and Islands architecture. You'll also learn how architectural patterns like MVC, MVP, and MVVM are useful from the perspective of a modern web application developer.

publications.title: Software Engineering - The Soft Parts (2022)
publications.description:
In "Software Engineering - The Soft Parts" Addy Osmani shares lessons from his first 10 years at Google on the "soft skills" that can help engineers become effective and scale their effectiveness. This guidance should help junior, mid-career and even senior developers move forward, deal with changing technology, and navigate building non-trivial systems.


Becoming a good engineer is about collecting experience. Each project, even small ones, is a chance to add new techniques and tools to your toolbox. Where this delivers even more value is when you can solve problems by pairing techniques learned on one project with tools learned working on another. This short book tries to capture what the "soft skills" are and how they can all add up.

publications.title: Learning Patterns - a JavaScript & React Design Patterns Book (2021, 2022)
publications.description:
Patterns.dev is a book on design patterns and component patterns for building powerful web apps with vanilla JavaScript and React. 

In this book, you will learn design patterns, performance and rendering patterns for building high-quality web applications using modern JavaScript and React.
Patterns are time-tested templates for writing code. They can be really powerful, whether you're a seasoned developer or beginner, bringing a valuable level of resilience and flexibility to your codebase.

Whether it's better user-experience, developer-experience or just smarter architecture, the patterns in “Learning Patterns” can be a valuable consideration for any modern web application.

publications.title: Image Optimization (2021)
publications.description:
Images have always been a key part of the web. Our brains are able to interpret images much faster than text, which is why high-quality visuals drive conversions and user engagement.

That said, loading images efficiently at scale isn’t a little project for a quiet afternoon. It requires understanding of compression techniques, loading behavior, image decoding and image CDNs, adaptive media loading and caching.

This book will equip you with everything you need to know to optimize how you compress, serve and maintain images — boosting performance along the way.

headline: Full-Stack + AI Engineer | Building Scalable Systems, AI Agents & LLM Workflows | Python, Java, TypeScript
about:
Hi, I’m Darryl.

I’m a software engineer who somehow went from investment banking to startups to building software for a living.

I started my career at Citi, where I spent my days building financial models, making PowerPoint slides, and learning that “urgent” is a highly subjective term. After catching the startup bug, I co-founded a few ventures, directed a short web series with more enthusiasm than budget, and eventually discovered that writing code was a far more scalable way to solve problems.

Since then, I’ve built across a fairly wide range of domains: fintech platforms, trading systems, onboarding and identity infrastructure, payments, internal tooling, digital assets, and now AI-native products.

The upside of taking a slightly chaotic route through finance, startups, and software is that I can usually understand why the business wants something, how the product should work, and why the database is on fire.

I enjoy working across the stack, but I naturally gravitate toward backend systems, platform architecture, and the kind of distributed systems problems that only reveal themselves after you’ve already deployed.

Lately, I’ve been spending a lot of time in the AI ecosystem.

While the world debates which model tops the benchmark leaderboard this week, I’m more interested in the practical questions:

How do we make AI systems reliable?
How do agents safely interact with tools and other agents?
How do we manage context, memory, evaluation, and observability?
How do we build AI products that create real business value instead of just impressive demos?

Current areas of interest:

- Agentic and multi-agent systems
- Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
- Context-Augmented Generation (CAG)
- Model Context Protocol (MCP)
- AI tooling and orchestration
- LLM evaluation, guardrails, and observability
- Production AI infrastructure

Tech I work with regularly:

- TypeScript / NestJS / React / Next.js
- Python / FastAPI
- Java / Spring Boot
- PostgreSQL / MySQL / Redis
- AWS / GCP / Terraform / Docker / Kubernetes

I enjoy building products, mentoring engineers, solving difficult technical problems, and occasionally deleting thousands of lines of code that seemed like a great idea six months earlier.
  
If you’re building something interesting in AI, fintech, developer tooling, or infrastructure, let’s connect.
  
GitHub: github.com/darrylwongqz

currentPosition.position: Member of Technical Staff | Applied AI Engineering | Forward Deployed Engineer
currentPosition.description:
Building a ton of AI applications while drinking a lot more coffee than previous roles and lashing my figurative whip on multiple codexes every day.

Vibe code cleanup specialist across the stack - frontend, backend, infra & deployed across multiple client projects in the region.

Owner/lead engineer of multiple R&D workstreams that include:
- Built the Fullstack Template - a developer starter kit that automatically integrates various services with CICD - that the entire firm uses as a base to build agentic applications from and shipping production ready AI applications in days (sometimes hours) not weeks.
- Sumi - internal prototyping tool primarily for delivery leads and non-technical stakeholders to build apps fast while minimising tech debt when handing projects over to engineers to scale and take to production.

multiLocaleHeadline.headline: Full-Stack + AI Engineer | Building Scalable Systems, AI Agents & LLM Workflows | Python, Java, TypeScript

experience.position: Member of Technical Staff | Applied AI Engineering | Forward Deployed Engineer
experience.description:
Building a ton of AI applications while drinking a lot more coffee than previous roles and lashing my figurative whip on multiple codexes every day.

Vibe code cleanup specialist across the stack - frontend, backend, infra & deployed across multiple client projects in the region.

Owner/lead engineer of multiple R&D workstreams that include:
- Built the Fullstack Template - a developer starter kit that automatically integrates various services with CICD - that the entire firm uses as a base to build agentic applications from and shipping production ready AI applications in days (sometimes hours) not weeks.
- Sumi - internal prototyping tool primarily for delivery leads and non-technical stakeholders to build apps fast while minimising tech debt when handing projects over to engineers to scale and take to production.

Frontend, Backend, DevSecOps - did it all. Some say this is a full stack role, but full cycle engineer would be more apt.

Drank way too much coffee raising and assigning myself Jira tickets to the issues I found, triaged and solved. Fell short of being awarded a medal because the budget found its way to better use - pizzas and beer parties to boost team morale - did I mention we tokenized whiskies?

Jokes aside, learnt a lot, and I’m glad I was able to take on massive scope and responsibilities that I’m sure I would not be able to experience in most bigger firms.
Helping career switchers go from zero to full stack heroes!

Antler is a global early-stage venture capital firm that invests in the defining technology companies of tomorrow.

Rapidly tested start-up ideas with over a period of a few months. Worked with venture capitalists and industry specialists to find product-market-fit for these ideas... 

Basically a nice way of phrasing how I created lots of slide decks selling dreams to people with money with the hope that they buy into one of them and/or help create them together with me.

My first real shot at creating a tech-enabled startup where our team made a push for users to get rewarded while getting served more relevant ads.

Liaised with various big name publishers in the region in an effort to push our mutually beneficial unique UA strategy.

But really... the highlight of the whole experience was when I directed, produced and edited a cool web-series laced with deadpan humour as part of marketing: https://youtu.be/okKocxXv8iY

Ran even more Facebook Ads, this time for E-Commerce until Covid came.

Took a portion of the profits and invested in Shopify - made more money there ironically.

Then pivoted to mainly content creation. My career trajectory might have been completely different should I have had the guts to post dance videos on TikTok.

Oh did I mention that I learnt how to code during this period too?

Programmatic Advertising Specialist
I was there long enough to know that I should invest in the company.

And yes I invested.

Ran a bunch of Facebook ads, got a cut of the revenue selling other people's products.

I arguably got in at the last bit of time where affiliate marketing was easy to make money.

Debt Capital Markets & Leveraged Finance

Created lots of decks, tinkered with a lot of excel sheets, and found many reasons to justify volatile market movements with the US president's twitter account retroactively.

“Always a good time to raise” was the motto instilled by my bosses and reflected in the pitch books that I ended up getting staffed on till 3-4am in the mornings every other day.

Also wrote a lot of credit memos and also managed to discover many synonyms and phrases to describe the same thing 5x over to show our risk committee that we did our homework and due diligence

I now know a lot about the plastic surgery industry because I was made to do a comprehensive deck on it.

Biggest takeaway is to not do fillers, they stay in your face way longer than you think and not in places you want.

Equity Capital Markets

Stared at prospectuses all day. Had a lot of $2 Old Tea Hut Coffee.

Greatest contribution would be providing my team free access to CapitalIQ that my university paid for (well I guess I paid for it via my exorbitant school fees).

Learnt how to use the SAP system and chased other companies to pay up their dues in a timely fashion.

I got really good at folding pink slips and shoving them into envelopes.

Could recite the SAP codes for accounts receivable in my sleep for a while.

education.description:
Contrary to what a lot of people think about college, I did learn a lot about investing and finance there from professors who actually put their money where their mouths are.
Also the best time of my life that I will forever treasure.

2 years went by so fast it almost felt like it didn't happen.
Also a time where I could bury my head into books almost 24/7 without complaining.
education.insights:
Grade: 6 Distinctions
Activities and societies: Anderson Junior College Academic Scholar, A'STAR (JC) Science Award, Table Tennis Club

Activities and societies: Learnt to code. Met great people. Really enjoyed my time diving deep into programming. Also a time where I thought I wrote good code, but really wrote so much spagetti I could pass off as an Italian.

experience.description: Creating AI powered intelligent systems to automate development, quality assurance and other SaaS operational activities.

Distributed Systems: Spearheaded the migration of a legacy batch monolith into a distributed master-worker system; achieved zero unplanned downtime over 3+ months.

Scalable Development: Engineered a fault-tolerant, asynchronous SFTP system (Spring Boot, JS) that scaled to 320K+ monthly invoices, eliminating 110+ hours of manual workload.

Security & Compliance: Secured a supply chain handling PHI for 1M+ individuals; reached 100% audit compliance via custom patches and critical library upgrades.

Mentorship: Trained and mentored Associate Software Engineers on system architecture and clean code practices.

Production Integration: Rapidly integrated into a large-scale SaaS codebase, contributing production-ready features in Java, Spring, and SQL within the first 90 days.

Feature Development: Built a multi-threaded job management system using concurrent queues, eliminating manual resets and saving ~20 engineering hours/month.

Infrastructure Monitoring: Co-authored a service monitoring system to monitor internal tools usage pattern.

headline: AI Research Engineer
about:
Talented software developer and mathematician, thriving on complex abstract technical problems.

20 years’ experience in software development: principally Java; also C++ and Python.

PhD in Machine Learning and Data Mining.

Track record of implementing dramatic performance enhancements, ranging from ten-fold to thousand-fold improvement.

Experienced line manager and development team leader.

Adept at technical presentation, with a flair for making complex topics accessible, for example:
•	Lexical/Syntactical analysis
•	Object-oriented programming without classes
•	Solving “Countdown” Numbers Games using dynamic programming
•	The Japanese language

Project Euler development team member; previously UK’s top competitor.

Created a compiler for a Java-like language, incorporating polymorphism, local variable capture, genericity, and type covariance.

Created a 3D graphics engine for rendering non-convex symmetric polyhedra.

experience.description:
Java development team leader for a market-leading scientific data management application in the pharmaceutical/biotechnology sector.

•	Line manager and team leader with responsibility for six developers
•	Search architecture, including ETL processes for updating ontology-driven search indices
•	Growing unit test coverage in legacy areas of code, as a pragmatic means of acquiring knowledge about those areas and improving their quality
•	The “go to” developer on internationalisation, including thorny problems in time zones and text encodings
•	Detailed knowledge of advanced language features, in particular generics in Java and C#

Researched and implemented data mining and machine learning methods for Quantitative Structure Activity Relationships (QSARs):
•	Multivariate Linear Regression
•	Principal Components Analysis
•	Partial Least Squares
•	K-Means Clustering
•	Support Vector Machines
•	Random Decision Forests

The research-based nature of this role led to several publications and conference attendances, a patent, and ultimately to a PhD.
